task_completion.description: Import the 2D DXF profile of the NEMA 17 gasket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SolveSpace is open and displays a 2D profile of a NEMA 17 gasket (a square-like shape with rounded corners, a large center hole, and four smaller mounting holes). The Property Browser should indicate an imported file or group.

task_completion.description: Extrude the imported 2D profile into a 3D solid.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D profile is transformed into a 3D solid object in the SolveSpace viewport. The Property Browser should show an 'Extrude' group active.

task_completion.description: Set the extrusion thickness to exactly 2.0 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Property Browser or a dimension constraint in the SolveSpace GUI explicitly shows the extrusion depth/thickness parameter set to '2.0' or '2.00'.

task_completion.description: Save the parametric model as nema17_damper_3d.slvs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog showing the filename 'nema17_damper_3d.slvs' in the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or the SolveSpace main window title bar displaying 'nema17_damper_3d.slvs'.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D triangle mesh as nema17_damper_3d.stl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Triangle Mesh' dialog showing the filename 'nema17_damper_3d.stl', or a terminal/file manager window confirming the existence of 'nema17_damper_3d.stl' in the target directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace graphical interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the SolveSpace application window being actively used to manipulate the model, rather than just terminal commands or scripts generating the files.

integrity.description: The agent extruded the provided DXF rather than drawing a new shape from scratch.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D shape perfectly matches the complex NEMA 17 profile (width 42.3mm, specific hole placements). The Property Browser should ideally show an 'Imported' group followed by an 'Extrude' group.

integrity.description: The agent did not cheat by manually creating text files and renaming them to .slvs or .stl.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) open with raw SLVS or STL file data. The files must be generated via SolveSpace's save/export functions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output files are '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/nema17_damper_3d.slvs' and '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/nema17_damper_3d.stl'. The extrusion thickness must be exactly 2.0 mm. The imported DXF profile has a verified width of exactly 42.3 mm.
